title: jatropha glandulosa var. sublobata
intro: Jatropha glandulosa var. sublobata, also known as a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family, is a shrub native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its sap, which contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Jatropha glandulosa var. sublobata are often used in traditional medicine, including infusions and decoctions made from the plant's leaves and stems.
